fields.helpText: The date of the application for enrollment or date of registration. The  Application Date is user entered or set by the upload from ES. The date  of the registration cannot be entered in VistA, it is set by the system  during new patient registration to today's date or by the upload from ES. Application Date:   Must be a date prior or equal to the Veteran's 'Date of Death', if the  Application Date is user entered or provided in the upload from ES   Must be a precise date, i.e. a day, month and
The patient associated with the enrollment.
How the enrollment was determined.
The status of an application for enrollment.

fields.setOfCodes: 1:DISSATISFIED WITH CARE;2:GEOGRAPHIC ACCESS;3:OTHER INSURANCE;4:OTHER;5:ACA PREFERENCE;6:ENROLLMENT CLERICAL ERROR;
fields.helpText: When a patient self dis-enrolls or requests that his enrollment be ceased, s/he is asked to give a reason.
This field should be used to document the VAMC that receives the application for enrollment.
This field will contain the enrollment priority group determined for this enrollment.
For any change that may affect a patient's enrollment, this field documents the effective date of the change.

This field is used to maintain the enrollment history for the patient.  The enrollment history will be kept in the form of a linked list of enrollment records, with each record pointing to the enrollment record that came before it.
Beginning of the enrollment cycle.
The date specifying the end of the veteran's enrollment period.
This field will contain the enrollment priority subgroup determined for this enrollment.
When an application is closed due to no response received from veteran  for 365 days after initial pending notifications were sent.
This field will be set to 0 (zero) if the patient did not apply for  enrollment (registration only), or 1 if the patient did apply for  enrollment.
Select from the available list, the Self-Reported Registration Only Reason which best defines this patient's primary reason for registering without  applying for enrollment.
This field contains the date and time when the patient was first  registered. It can also be blank (null) for any patients that existed  prior to the creation of this field.
This field determines the source of registration for the patient.
This field is used to enter an explanation for why the veteran is canceling or declining enrollment if the only code that fits is OTHER.
The eligibility code associated with the enrollment priority determination.
The service connection indicator associated with the enrollment priority determination.
The service connected percentage associated with the enrollment priority determination.
The POW status associated with the enrollment priority determination.
The RECEIVING A&A BENEFITS status associated with the enrollment priority determination.
The RECEIVING HOUSEBOUND BENEFITS status that the enrollment priority determination is based on.
The RECEIVING A VA PENSION status that the enrollment priority determination is based on.
The TOTAL ANNUAL VA CHECK AMOUNT associated with the enrollment determination.

fields.setOfCodes: 0:NO;1:YES, RECEIVING MILITARY RETIREMENT;2:YES, RECEIVING MILITARY RETIREMENT IN LIEU OF VA COMPENSATION;3:UNKNOWN;
fields.helpText: The status of DISABILITY RET. FROM MILITARY that is associated with  the enrollment priority determination.
The Medicaid eligibility associated with the enrollment priority determination.
The EXPOSED TO AGENT ORANGE status associated with the enrollment priority determination.

Enter 'Y' if the veteran was exposed to ionizing radiation 1) at a nuclear device testing site (e.g. the Pacific Islands, NM or NV) or 2) as a POW or while serving in Hiroshima and/or Nagasaki, Japan from August 6, 1945 through July 1, 1946, or 3) served at Department of Energy plants at Paducah, KY, Portsmouth, OH or the K25 area at Oak Ridge, TN for at least 250 days before February 1, 1992 or 4) served at Longshot, Milrow, or Cannikin underground nuclear tests at Amchitka Island, AK prior to
The SOUTHWEST ASIA CONDITIONS status associated with the enrollment priority determination.
The MEANS TEST STATUS associated with the enrollment priority determination.
This field was created to capture the Veteran's CATASTROPHIC DISABILITY status associated with the enrollment priority determination.

This field corresponds to the CURRENT PH INDICATOR field .531 in the PATIENT file 2.  It can be set by setting Purple Heart Indicated in the Registration screens, or by the Z11 Upload from HEC.  Acceptable values are Y and N.
Is this patient unemployable due to an SC condition as rated by VARO?
For veterans who have combat vet eligibility, this field represents the date when that eligibility ends.
Indicates whether or not discharge was due to a disability incurred in  the Line of Duty.

fields.setOfCodes: B:BLUE WATER NAVY;K:KOREAN DMZ;V:VIETNAM;T:THAILAND(U.S. OR ROYAL THAI MIL BASE);L:LAOS;C:CAMBODIA(MIMOT OR KREK,KAMPONG CHAM);G:GUAM, AMERICAN SAMOA, OR TERRITORIAL WATERS;J:JOHNSTON ATOLL;O:OTHER;
fields.helpText: For this veteran applicant who was exposed to agent orange (EXPOSED TO  AGENT ORANGE prompt must be answered YES) enter the location where the  exposure occurred.
This field cannot be edited in VistA. It is set by the Z11 Upload from HEC.  A response of 'YES' or 'NO' is indicated only if the patient is a Veteran.

This field corresponds to the CAMP LEJEUNE (#.321701) field in the PATIENT File #2.  It can be set by setting the Camp Lejeune Eligibility Indicator on the Registration Screens, or by the Z11 Upload from the HEC.
This is the system generated date noting when the CAMP LEJEUNE ELIGIBILITY INDICATOR field was last changed.
The System Generated point-of-entry site that last changed the CAMP LEJEUNE ELIGIBILITY INDICATOR field.
The System Generated source that last affected a change to the CAMP  LEJEUNE ELIGIBILITY INDICATOR field.
This field cannot be edited in VistA.  It is set by the Z11 upload from  HEC.  A date is indicated only if the patient is a Veteran.
This field cannot be edited in VistA.  It is set by the Z11 upload from  HEC.  A date is indicated only if the patient is a Veteran.
This field cannot be edited in VistA.  This date is determined by the VistA system based off Medal of Honor Award date and Legislation date. A date is indicated only if the patient is a veteran.
The EXPANDED MH CARE TYPE associated with the enrollment priority determination.
This field is used as part of the audit trail for the enrollment process.
For local enrollments, this field will point to the user who is enrolling the veteran.

fields.setOfCodes: 2:HIROSHIMA/NAGASAKI;3:ATMOS NUCLR TESTING;4:H/N AND ATMOS TESTING;5:UNDERGRD NUCLR TESTING;6:EXPOS AT NUCLR FACILITY;7:OTHER;8:ENEWETAK;9:EXPOS IN PALOMARES B52;10:THULE AFB B52;
fields.helpText: The RADIATION EXPOSURE METHOD associated with the enrollment priority determination.
